MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-03-2008 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Nov-03-2008 Vacated
MINI MINUTES: Dept. 218, Comm: William R. Gargano, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley.

This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Dept. 206 for Settlement on the record (settled by the JPT in Dept. 207).

Michael Heath, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Bin Chen, also present.
Defendant Tom Lee present in pro per.

Defendant agreed in open court to have a Judgment entered against him in the amount of $40,000.00.
Mr. Heath will prepared a Judgment for signature.
Matter was deemed settled, trial vacated.

OTHER SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-31-2008 Off Calendar

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-20-2008 Continued To Oct-31-08 At 09:15 A.M. In Dept 604

MISCELLANEOUS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-28-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Order to Show Cause to Defendant in Propria Persona for failure to comply with San Francisco Superior Court Early Settlement Program Policies and Procedures No. 4. (Administrative fee) is ordered off calendar/Fee waived. Proceedings reported by: Karen Farnsworth, CSR# 5162. (212)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, To Deem Matters Admitted, Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-25-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff Bin Chen's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, To Deem Matters Admitted, And For Sanctions.
Motion to compel discovery responses and to deem matters admitted GRANTED:
1. Defendant LEE shall serve full, complete and verified responses to plaintiff's Form Interrogatories [Set One] within ten days of this order. All objections are waived except attorney-client and attorney work product privilege;
2. Defendant LEE shall serve full, complete and verified responses to plaintiff's Request for Production of Documents within ten days of this order. All objections are waived except attorney-client and attorney work product privilege;
3. The matters set forth in plaintiff's Requests for Admissions Nos. 1 through 17, inclusive, are deemed admitted.
4. Request for sanctions granted. Sanctions shall issue in the sum of $250.00; imposition of sanctions stayed for ten days. If defendant complies with this order as set forth, imposition of sanctions shall be permanently stayed. COMMISSIONER PAUL SLAVIT. Not Reported

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-28-2008 Set For Trial On Nov-03-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Oct-17-08 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 604
